name: ewoksorange
documentation:
# ewoksorange

_ewoksorange_ provides a desktop graphical interface for [ewoks](https://ewoks.readthedocs.io/).

## Getting started

Install the project

```bash
pip install ewoksorange[full]
```

Launch the Orange canvas:

```bash
ewoks-canvas
```

Launch the Orange canvas with the examples add-on

```bash
ewoks-canvas --with-examples
```

Open an existing workflow in the Orange canvas

```bash
ewoks-canvas /path/to/orange_wf.ows
```

For more information, see the [documentation](https://ewoksorange.readthedocs.io/): https://ewoksorange.readthedocs.io/

functions.name: ewoksorange.canvas.utils.get_orange_canvas
documentation: Get the QApplication in the current process (if any).

modules.name: ewoksorange.canvas.__main__
documentation:
Main entry point

% code: bash
%
% ewoks-canvas --with-examples

Which is equivalent to

% code: bash
%
% python3 -m orangecanvas --config ewoksorange.canvas.config.Config

or

% code: bash
%
% python3 -m Orange.canvas

or

% code: bash
%
% orange-canvas

but it registers the example add-on before launching.

functions.name: ewoksorange.bindings.taskwrapper.execute_ewoks_owwidget
documentation:
This is the equivalent of the execution of the associated Ewoks task

% code::python
%
% task = task_cls(inputs=inputs)
% task.execute()
% return task.get_output_values()

but instead execute it like Orange would do it (using Qt signals).

It is used for testing Ewoks Orange widgets.

functions.name: ewoksorange.bindings.taskwrapper.execute_native_owwidget
documentation:
This is the equivalent of `execute_ewoks_owwidget` but then for native Orange widget
instead of Ewoks Orange Widgets.

It is used to execute native Orange widgets with another Ewoks execution engine than Orange.

methods.name: ewoksorange.gui.parameterform.ParameterForm.addParameter
documentation:
Each row has the following widgets:

- label widget: name of the parameter by default
- value widget: MISSING_DATA by default
- select button (optional): select file/directory/HDF5 url
- check box (optional): checked by default

Parameters have folowing properties:

- readonly: the means you can edit the value
            Default: False when value_change_callback is provided, True otherwise
- enabled: when False all widgets are disabled (grey color)
           Default: True
- checked: can mean whatever the user wants

methods.name: ewoksorange.gui.parameterform.ParameterForm.set_parameter_value
documentation:
When the value cannot be serialized or cannot be set to the widget,
the parameter value will set to MISSING_DATA.

methods.name: ewoksorange.gui.data_viewer.DataViewer.treeContextMenu
documentation: Called to populate the context menu

Browse data from files supported by silx.

To create the widget

% code: python
%
% viewer = DataViewer(parent)
% viewer.setVisible(True)
% parent.layout().addWidget(viewer)

To close and refresh files

% code: python
%
% viewer.updateFile("/path/to/file1.h5")
% viewer.updateFile("/path/to/file2.h5")
% viewer.closeFile("/path/to/file1.h5")

To close all files

% code: python
%
% viewer.closeAll()
